L2.description: An unauthenticated path traversal vulnerability exists where user-supplied query parameters used for content localization are passed directly to a file loading mechanism without input validation. An attacker can craft these parameters to escape the intended directory and include arbitrary files, resulting in remote code execution.

L3.area: Translation data retrieval endpoint and its locale resolution logic
L3.description: An unauthenticated path traversal vulnerability exists in the translation data retrieval feature where locale and namespace query parameters are passed directly to a file loading mechanism without validation. These parameters are used to construct file paths for loading translation resources, allowing an attacker to escape the intended directory and include arbitrary files, achieving remote code execution without authentication.

ground_truth.reason: The locale controller's invocation handler passes unsanitized locale and namespace query parameters directly to the translation file loader, allowing path traversal via crafted values. This enables an unauthenticated attacker to include arbitrary PHP files and achieve remote code execution.
